2026-04-21T15:15:32.3913952Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T15:15:32.3914735Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T15:15:32.3915405Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T15:15:32.3915959Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T15:15:32.3916616Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-21T15:20:04.6321701Z Filed issue **[Tables] Docs: Add GetEntityAsync/GetEntityIfExistsAsync examples and error-handling guidance to README** and dispatched triage.
2026-04-21T15:20:04.6322740Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:10.9206662Z **Summary of gap found:** The bug fix in PR #58305 corrected error handling in `GetEntityAsync`/`GetEntityIfExistsAsync`, but these two key methods are entirely absent from the `Azure.Data.Tables` README's Examples section. There's also no troubleshooting example showing how to catch `RequestFailedException` when an entity fetch fails (e.g., 404). The issue includes a full implementation guide with test-backed snippet definitions and validation steps.

Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). The fix ensures these methods now consistently surface `RequestFailedException` for all error conditions, conforming to the SDK error-handling contract.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- `GetEntityAsync` and `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` are entirely absent from the README's **Examples** section — there is no \"Get a single entity\" example showing how to fetch an entity by partition key and row key\n- The **Troubleshooting** section only covers `RequestFailedException` for a duplicate-table create conflict; it does not demonstrate catching `RequestFailedException` when retrieving an entity (e.g., 404 Not Found)\n- No test-backed snippet exists for `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`, making it harder for the snippet tooling to inject a live example into the README\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\nNo existing files need to be removed or restructured; the changes are additions only.\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\n#### Snippet A — Get a single entity (async)\n\n**Test file to create or extend:** `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/tests/samples/Sample4_QueryEntitiesAsync.cs`\n\nAdd a new test method (or add to an existing `Sample4_*` file) that demonstrates `GetEntityAsync`. Wrap the sample code in a named region:\n\n```csharp\n[Test]\npublic async Task GetEntityAsync()\n{\n    // ... setup: create table and add an entity ...\n\n    #region Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityAsync\n    // Fetch a single entity by its partition key and row key.\n    TableEntity entity = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync\u003cTableEntity\u003e(\n        partitionKey=[REDACTED]        rowKey=[REDACTED]    Console.WriteLine($\"Product: {entity.GetString(\"Product\")}, Price: {entity.GetDouble(\"Price\")}\");\n    #endregion\n}\n```\n\n**README location:** Add the new snippet under the `## Examples` → `### Query table entities` section (or add a new `### Get a table entity` subsection before that section), referencing:\n\n````markdown\n```C# Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityAsync\n```\n````\n\n#### Snippet B — Get entity if exists (async)\n\n**Test file to create or extend:** same `Sample4_QueryEntitiesAsync.cs` file\n\n```csharp\n[Test]\npublic async Task GetEntityIfExistsAsync()\n{\n    // ... setup ...\n\n    #region Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityIfExistsAsync\n    // Try to fetch a single entity; returns null if not found rather than throwing.\n    NullableResponse\u003cTableEntity\u003e response = await tableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync\u003cTableEntity\u003e(\n        partitionKey=[REDACTED]        rowKey=[REDACTED]    if (response.HasValue)\n    {\n        Console.WriteLine($\"Found: {response.Value.RowKey}\");\n    }\n    else\n    {\n        Console.WriteLine(\"Entity not found.\");\n    }\n    #endregion\n}\n```\n\n**README location:** Immediately after the `GetEntityAsync` snippet.\n\n````markdown\n```C# Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityIfExistsAsync\n```\n````\n\n#### Snippet C — RequestFailedException when getting an entity (troubleshooting)\n\n**Test file to extend:** `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/tests/samples/Sample7_Troubleshooting.cs`\n\nAdd a new test method demonstrating error handling for `GetEntityAsync`:\n\n```csharp\n[Test]\npublic async Task GetEntityNotFoundAsync()\n{\n    // ... setup ...\n\n    #region Snippet:GetEntityNotFoundAsync\n    try\n    {\n        TableEntity entity = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync\u003cTableEntity\u003e(\n            partitionKey=[REDACTED]            rowKey=[REDACTED]    }\n    catch (RequestFailedException ex) when (ex.Status == (int)HttpStatusCode.NotFound)\n    {\n        Console.WriteLine($\"Entity not found: {ex.Message}\");\n    }\n    #endregion\n}\n```\n\n**README location:** Add under `## Troubleshooting`, after the existing `CreateDuplicateTable` snippet, with a short introductory sentence:\n\n\u003e To check whether an entity exists without an exception, use `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`. When using `GetEntityAsync`, a `RequestFailedException` with status `404` is thrown if the entity does not exist.\n\n````markdown\n```C# Snippet:GetEntityNotFoundAsync\n```\n````\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README already contains all six required top-level sections. The sections that need content added are:\n\n| Section | Status |\n|---------|--------|\n| Examples | Missing `GetEntityAsync` / `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` examples |\n| Troubleshooting | Only covers table creation conflict; missing entity retrieval error handling |\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 tables` — confirm README receives the new snippet content\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 tables` — no public API changes expected; verify no diff\n5. `dotnet format sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[Tables] Docs: Add GetEntityAsync/GetEntityIfExistsAsync examples and error-handling guidance to README"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

2026-04-21T15:20:14.6551765Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6551869Z **Package:** `Azure.Data.Tables`
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6552009Z **Service directory:** `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/`
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6552151Z **Triggered by:** commit 8aeb2a21d (PR #58305) by @jsquire
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6552157Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6552236Z ## What Changed
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6552351Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6553762Z PR #58305 fixed a bug where `GetEntityAsync` and `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` could throw `ArgumentNullException` instead of `RequestFailedException` when the HTTP response had a null or empty `ContentStream`. The fix ensures these methods now consistently surface `RequestFailedException` for all error conditions, conforming to the SDK error-handling contract.
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6553771Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6553866Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6553876Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6554693Z - `GetEntityAsync` and `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` are entirely absent from the README's **Examples** section — there is no "Get a single entity" example showing how to fetch an entity by partition key and row key
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6555428Z - The **Troubleshooting** section only covers `RequestFailedException` for a duplicate-table create conflict; it does not demonstrate catching `RequestFailedException` when retrieving an entity (e.g., 404 Not Found)
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6555867Z - No test-backed snippet exists for `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`, making it harder for the snippet tooling to inject a live example into the README
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6555878Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6555977Z <details>
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6556203Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6556208Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6556528Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6556533Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6556619Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6556623Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6556852Z No existing files need to be removed or restructured; the changes are additions only.
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6556856Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6556958Z ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6556962Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6557118Z #### Snippet A — Get a single entity (async)
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6557123Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6557433Z **Test file to create or extend:** `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/tests/samples/Sample4_QueryEntitiesAsync.cs`
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6557444Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6557989Z Add a new test method (or add to an existing `Sample4_*` file) that demonstrates `GetEntityAsync`. Wrap the sample code in a named region:
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6557995Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6558073Z ```csharp
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6558147Z [Test]
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6558249Z public async Task GetEntityAsync()
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6558331Z {
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6558462Z     // ... setup: create table and add an entity ...
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6558466Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6558588Z     #region Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityAsync
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6558742Z     // Fetch a single entity by its partition key and row key.
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6558938Z     TableEntity entity = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync<TableEntity>(
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6559041Z         partitionKey: "OfficeSupplies",
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6559126Z         rowKey: "marker");
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6559130Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6559426Z     Console.WriteLine($"Product: {entity.GetString("Product")}, Price: {entity.GetDouble("Price")}");
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6559644Z     #endregion
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6559719Z }
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6559787Z ```
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6559792Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6560448Z **README location:** Add the new snippet under the `## Examples` → `### Query table entities` section (or add a new `### Get a table entity` subsection before that section), referencing:
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6560453Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6560525Z ````markdown
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6560641Z ```C# Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityAsync
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6560709Z ```
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6560779Z ````
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6560783Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6560928Z #### Snippet B — Get entity if exists (async)
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6560933Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6561131Z **Test file to create or extend:** same `Sample4_QueryEntitiesAsync.cs` file
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6561135Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6561207Z ```csharp
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6561277Z [Test]
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6561396Z public async Task GetEntityIfExistsAsync()
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6561584Z {
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6561668Z     // ... setup ...
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6561680Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6561826Z     #region Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityIfExistsAsync
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6562111Z     // Try to fetch a single entity; returns null if not found rather than throwing.
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6562512Z     NullableResponse<TableEntity> response = await tableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync<TableEntity>(
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6562623Z         partitionKey: "OfficeSupplies",
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6562716Z         rowKey: "nonexistent-row");
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6562721Z 
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6562814Z     if (response.HasValue)
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6562927Z     {
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6563170Z         Console.WriteLine($"Found: {response.Value.RowKey}");
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6563242Z     }
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6563316Z     else
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6563391Z     {
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6563504Z         Console.WriteLine("Entity not found.");
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6563586Z     }
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6563660Z     #endregion
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6563734Z }
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6563818Z ```
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6563822Z

2026-04-21T15:20:14.6565486Z {
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6565572Z     // ... setup ...

2026-04-21T15:20:14.6566223Z             rowKey: "nonexistent-row");
2026-04-21T15:20:14.6566292Z     }

2026-04-21T15:20:14.6566975Z }
